title: Must-have seasoning + 13-inch M1 MacBook resale listing — Apr 2022
html:
TL;DR

  • Two unrelated layers in one note. Layer 1: a list of curiosity-prompts Yahir likely planned to ask people — "what's a must-have seasoning (no salt or pepper)," "stranded on an island, one item, why," "kitchen fire — what's the first thing you do," "fair criteria to be waterboarded," "UFC walk-out song, replicate?", "what would a dangerous-intruder drill look like."
  • Layer 2: a copy-paste of his own MacBook resale listing — 13-inch M1 MacBook Pro w/ 16GB RAM + 512GB SSD + AppleCare till 2023, paid full retail (~$1968), reason: "need for a bigger screen."
  • The seasoning prompt list is an early instance of Yahir's piercing prompts practice — using off-axis questions to surface character.
  • Prefigures the m1 mac purchase log (which lives in 2021 09 15 m1 mac buy list) — this is the other end of that machine's lifecycle, six months in, already being sold.

Key prompts

"What is a fair criteria to be waterboarded?"

"Walk-out song in the UFC, replicate?"

Why it matters in the graph

  • Predates and validates the 2025 piercing prompts note as a multi-year pattern, not a one-off.
  • The MacBook is the same M1 from 2021 09 15 m1 mac buy list — the listing is evidence the unit was upgraded out of by spring 2022 (presumably for a 14"/16" Pro).
  • Pairs with 2022 08 30 untitled singapore laptop budget (mid-2024 he's again computing whether to buy a MacBook in Singapore) — Yahir cycles through laptops more than most.

Open questions

  • Did the MacBook actually sell? At what price?
  • What replaced it — a Pro 14"/16"?
  • Were these prompts ever actually asked at the OSU table or are they one-way to-self?
